{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks whether combining antiretroviral agents in a single dosage form enhances the quality of life of HIV/AIDS patients. Multiple documents support that fixed-dose combinations (e.g., abacavir/lamivudine, abacavir/dolutegravir/lamivudine) are used to improve patient compliance. Document [1] states that combination medications with once-daily dosing are preferred because they increase patient compliance up to three times over more complex regimens. Document [12] notes that combining three to four drugs into one pill improves adherence and long-term effectiveness. Document [18] specifically mentions that treatment with efavirenz can improve quality of life, but its efficacy depends on adherence, and adherence is improved with simpler regimens. Document [27] emphasizes that interprofessional teamwork and patient education are critical to maximizing adherence, which directly impacts quality of life. Since improved adherence leads to better viral suppression, reduced side effects, and better immune function, all of which contribute to enhanced quality of life, the evidence strongly supports that single-dose combinations enhance quality of life. Therefore, the answer is 'yes'.", "answer_choice": "A"}
